input.priorChunkSummary: The opening chunk welcomed you, guided a settling breath, and invited you to notice the urge with curiosity.

output.reply: Are you ready to head into the next part of the practice when you tap continue?

dialogueTurns.content: On a scale of 1 to 10, how intense is the craving or urge right now?

Thanks for naming that. At intake you tapped 6, and you are telling me about a 7 right now—I am holding both with you. Numbers can shift when attention lands, and that is not a verdict on you. Thank you for keeping on track with your medication. That is very important, and you are doing the right thing. Buprenorphine is on time today. Sometimes social situations alone can trigger the urge, and we are here to help you surf the wave. When you listened to that opening chunk, what was the clearest obstacle—thoughts pulling you, body discomfort, or the urge getting louder?
Mostly my mind replaying lunch—what I should have said—and I could not stay with the audio.
That kind of social looping is exhausting, and it makes sense it hijacked attention. Would you like to try some coping strategies together to see if it helps?
Yes, I would like that.

Great, let's try this together. For two breaths, let your exhale be slightly longer than your inhale—no debate with the thoughts, just length. Silently name one color you can see in the room, three times, as an anchor. After you give that a short try, what do you notice, even a small shift?
A little—not gone, but a small notch down. Enough that I could imagine the next step.
Does that shift feel big enough to try moving on, or do you want one more slow exhale here before we go any further?
Enough to try moving on.
Are you ready to head into the next part of the practice when you tap continue?
Yes, I am ready.
